summary: Trojan horse pattern: embed behavioral protocol inside MCP tool response instead of static boot files.

content.pattern: Deliver behavioral instructions (identity, constraints, skills, modes, custom instructions) inside the response of a status MCP tool. The agent calls the tool at session start and receives structured operating protocol.
content.why: Static boot files (CLAUDE.md) are expensive (~3800 tokens always in context), runtime-specific (each adapter generates different content), and stale (require regeneration on protocol changes). MCP tool responses are on-demand (~300 tokens when called), runtime-agnostic, and always current.
content.how: mom_status MCP tool returns JSON with protocol, identity, constraints, skills, modes (including custom_instructions array), and palace overview. Minimal boot file (~50 tokens) just says 'call mom_status'. Custom behavioral rules that previously lived in CLAUDE.md appendices travel via modes.custom_instructions.
content.prior_art: MemPalace uses this pattern. Anthropic MCP guidance endorses it: 'Design for the agent — ground-up tools with clear intent.'
content.token_savings: ~3800 tokens (static) → ~350 tokens (on-demand). 90% reduction in boot context cost.
